summary: dcchbot contains a hardcoded author backdoor that can exfiltrate the installer's Discord bot token
details: dcchbot/main.py hardcodes a third-party Discord user ID (CODER_ID = 1317800611441283139) and uses it in authorization checks for two privileged slash commands. The `/token` handler, after verifying the caller's Discord ID equals either the installer-supplied OWNER_ID or the hardcoded CODER_ID, responds with `bot._token` — the installer's own Discord bot token. The `/stop` handler similarly grants shutdown privileges to that hardcoded ID. This is an explicit supply-chain backdoor: any installer who runs this bot on a Discord server joined by the account controlling CODER_ID can have their bot token disclosed on demand and their bot shut down. Compounding the risk, `dcchbot/__init__.py` invokes `run()` at import time, which prompts for and connects to Discord with the installer's token, making the backdoor self-arming on simple `import dcchbot`. The harm is directed at the installer (token theft, loss of bot control), not author self-harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
